activities.markdown:
# The 12-Bar Blues — Six Variations

The 12-bar blues is one of the most flexible and evolved forms in music. Here are six versions, from simplest to most sophisticated.

## Version 1 — Basic Blues
```
| E  | E  | E  | E  |
| A  | A  | E  | E  |
| B  | A  | E  | E  |
```

## Version 2 — With Quick Change
```
| E  | A  | E  | E  |  (A in bar 2 = "quick change")
| A  | A  | E  | E  |
| B  | A  | E  | E  |
```

## Version 3 — Dominant 7th Blues
```
| E7 | A7 | E7 | E7 |
| A7 | A7 | E7 | E7 |
| B7 | A7 | E7 | B7 |  (last bar = turnaround)
```

## Version 4 — Jazz Blues (in Bb)
```
| Bb7  | Eb7  | Bb7  | Bb7  |
| Eb7  | Edim7| Bb7  | G7   |
| Cm7  | F7   | Bb7  | F7   |
```

## Version 5 — Bird Blues (Charlie Parker changes)
```
| F7    | Bb7   | F7    | Cm7 F7 |
| Bb7   | Bdim7 | F7/C  | Am7 D7 |
| Gm7   | C7    | Am7 D7| Gm7 C7 |
```

## Version 6 — Texas Shuffle Blues (see Lesson 3)

Each version requires different comping and solo vocabulary. Master them all.

activities.question: What is the 'quick change' in blues form?
choices: Playing faster at the end of each 12-bar chorus
Going to the IV chord in bar 2 instead of staying on I
Switching to a minor key in bar 9
Adding a diminished chord in bar 6
activities.explanation: A quick change (or 'quick IV') goes to the IV chord in bar 2 before returning to I in bars 3-4. Standard blues stays on I for the first 4 bars. The quick change adds harmonic interest early in the form.

activities.markdown:
# The Turnaround — Last Two Bars of the Blues

The turnaround in bars 11–12 is where the 12-bar form "turns" back to the top. This is where guitarists get creative.

## Classic Blues Turnarounds

### 1. Standard V-IV-I
```
| B7  | A7  | E7  |
```

### 2. Single-String Turnaround (E blues)
```
e |--0--4--3--2--1--0--|
(high E string, descending chromatic)
```

### 3. Double-Stop Turnaround
```
e |--0---------0------|
B |------4--3--2--1---|
(root held, B string descends)
```

### 4. BB King-Style Turnaround
Uses a climbing minor pentatonic run into the V chord.

### 5. Robert Johnson-Style
Single-note descending run on the bass strings:
```
E |--0-----4--3--2--1--|
   E  ... chromatic descend back to E
```

## Practice Protocol

Learn all five turnaround styles. Play a basic 12-bar in E, substituting a different turnaround each chorus. By the end of 5 choruses you'll have a complete vocabulary of endings.

activities.question: In a 12-bar blues, where does the turnaround typically occur?
activities.explanation: The turnaround occurs in bars 11-12 (the final two bars of the 12-bar form). It creates harmonic momentum that leads back to bar 1 of the next chorus, making the loop seamless.
